data.body:
=== EXPERIMENT RESULT (round 2): Opus-vs-Sonnet model tradeoff + intentional grouping (460 runs) ===

Follow-up to the chunking/budget grounding. Answers: (1) how many criteria can OPUS handle/turn without
degradation; (2) $/criterion Opus-vs-Sonnet at each model's max stable N; (3) does intentional COHERENT grouping
raise Sonnet's reliable ceiling. 460 calls, 0 errors, ≈$22.7 (Opus $16.3 + Sonnet $6.3). Pricing (authoritative,
via claude-api skill): Opus 4.8 $5/$25 per M, Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15 per M (Opus ~1.67×/token). Writeup committed at
docs/experiments/plan-review-model-tradeoff.md; raw data + harnesses under docs/experiments/data/
(runs3_opus.jsonl, runs4_group.jsonl, batch3_opus.py, batch4_grouping.py, analyze3.py). Thinking OFF for both
models (like-for-like). Exp A = 5 tickets × N{2,4,6,8,12} × 2 random partitions × 2 repeats on Opus (mirrors the
Sonnet batch2). Exp B = 6 tickets × {coherent, anti} grouping × N{4,6} × 3 repeats on Sonnet, vs batch2's random.

R1 — OPUS DOES NOT DEGRADE AS N GROWS; SONNET DOES.
Pooled run-to-run consistency (Jaccard of FAIL-sets): Sonnet 0.70(N2)→0.53→0.47→0.56→0.59(N12) — peaks at 2,
falls after ~3. Opus 0.61→0.61→0.60→0.56→0.67 — FLAT-to-rising, most consistent at N=12. So Opus's reliable
ceiling ≈ the full set (~12 criteria/turn); Sonnet's ≈ 2–3. Opus also avoids Sonnet's big-plan flip (container
epic @N=12: Sonnet 3↔0 flips J0.33; Opus stable). Caveat: neither is uniformly stable at 12 on EVERY plan (Opus
noisier on the complex leaf @12, J0.42; returns 0 on the container epic — stable but maybe under-flagging). Keep
chunk <12 on the LARGEST plans for both; the flat-vs-falling SHAPE is the transferable result.

R2 — ONE OPUS CALL BEATS MANY RELIABLE SONNET CALLS, ON COST *AND* RELIABILITY.
$/criterion at each model's stable setting: Sonnet @N=2 (stable region) $0.0123/crit, $0.147/review (6 calls,
plan re-sent 6×). Opus @N=12 (stable max) $0.0086/crit, $0.103/review (1 call, plan sent once). The per-TOKEN
premium (1.67×) INVERTS per REVIEW because Opus amortizes the plan+system once while a reliable Sonnet review
needs 4–6 small calls. → One Opus pass is ~30% CHEAPER per criterion than reliable Sonnet AND more consistent.
Sonnet is cheaper in absolute terms only at N=12 ($0.057/review) — the config that's UNSTABLE on big plans. So
"many cheap Sonnet agents" is a FALSE ECONOMY at the reliable setting.

R3 — COHERENT GROUPING ~TRIPLES SONNET'S RELIABLE CRITERIA-PER-TURN.
Sonnet consistency by grouping: coherent 0.71(N4)/0.70(N6); random 0.53/0.47; anti 0.79/0.66. COHERENT@6 (0.70)
MATCHES RANDOM@2 (0.70) → clustering criteria by the plan facet they examine lets Sonnet reliably review ~6
criteria/turn at the consistency random only reaches at 2 (~3× lift), WITHOUT suppressing findings (coherent
flag-rate 0.25–0.26 ≈ random 0.25–0.27). Anti-affinity also raises Jaccard but partly by UNDER-flagging (0.21 →
recall loss) — not a clean win. MECHANISM/CAVEAT: part of random's lower consistency is that its instances mix 2
DIFFERENT partitions (grouping varies); coherent/anti hold ONE fixed partition across repeats. That IS the
actionable finding: the gate should use a FIXED, declared grouping, not random chunking — fixing the partition is
most of the win, affinity clustering preserves recall on top.

RECOMMENDATIONS (fold into epic 5fd2 §6 + chunking-config schema):
(1) MODEL-TIERED CHUNKING: frontier (Opus) → up to 12/turn (1 call); mid (Sonnet) → 6/turn IF coherently grouped,
    else ≤3; weak/local → ≤3. (R1's "Sonnet ≤3" holds only for UNGROUPED chunking.)
(2) PREFER ONE OPUS PASS as the high-reliability default — cheaper per criterion than reliable Sonnet + more
    consistent. Reserve Sonnet small-chunk for the cost floor on small/simple plans (coherent N=6, $0.079/review).
(3) CHUNKING CONFIG MUST SPECIFY A FIXED, COHERENT PARTITION (criteria clustered by plan facet) — not random.
(4) BUDGET (updated): generous cap ≈ $0.30 / ~25k tokens per plan; typical Opus one-pass ≈ $0.10 / 9–15k tokens;
    typical reliable Sonnet ≈ $0.08–0.15. INDETERMINATE triggers unchanged.

CAVEATS carry over: DSO=one style; plan-text-only (codebase-grounded E4/G1G2/A1 barely fire); no ground-truth
labels (consistency is the trustworthy axis); thinking OFF (adaptive would shift quality+cost, untested); weak/
local model ceiling still unmeasured.
